message:
fix: close orphan step wisps before closing root molecule (gt-3o59)

Formula step wisps were created by pourDogMolecule but never fully closed,
leaking ~40 open wisps per 5-minute cycle across all patrols.

Root cause: dogMol.close() only closed the root wisp, leaving child step
wisps orphaned. Individual callers also had gaps:

- wisp_reaper: never closed "auto-close" step (also unmapped in discoverSteps)
- compactor_dog: used wrong slug "scan" (should be "inspect"), never closed "verify"
- jsonl_git_backup: never closed "verify" step

Fix has two layers:
1. Systemic: close() now calls closeRemainingSteps() which queries all children
   and closes any still-open ones before closing the root. This backstop
   prevents leaks regardless of individual caller bugs.
2. Per-caller: added missing closeStep calls and fixed wrong slugs.
3. discoverSteps: added keyword mappings for "auto-close", "sync", "offsite".

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -114,10 +114,12 @@ func (d *Daemon) runCompactorDog() {
 	databases := d.compactorDatabases()
 	if len(databases) == 0 {
 		d.logger.Printf("compactor_dog: no databases to compact")
-		mol.failStep("scan", "no databases found")
+		mol.failStep("inspect", "no databases found")
 		return
 	}
 
+	mol.closeStep("inspect")
+
 	compacted := 0
 	skipped := 0
 	errors := 0
@@ -167,6 +169,8 @@ func (d *Daemon) runCompactorDog() {
 		mol.closeStep("compact")
 	}
 
+	mol.closeStep("verify")
+
 	d.logger.Printf("compactor_dog: cycle complete — compacted=%d skipped=%d errors=%d",
 		compacted, skipped, errors)
 	mol.closeStep("report")
